title: Computational Geometry in C
backmatter: chapter 9 is a source-map and reading-graph unit

units.title: Polygon Triangulation
units.focus: Art gallery theorems, polygon area, segment intersection, ears, and triangulation implementation.
units.label: Chapter 1

units.title: Polygon Partitioning
units.focus: Monotone partitioning, trapezoidalization, mountains, linear triangulation, and convex partitioning.
units.label: Chapter 2

units.title: Convex Hulls in Two Dimensions
units.focus: Convexity, extreme points, gift wrapping, QuickHull, Graham scan, lower bounds, and divide-and-conquer.
units.label: Chapter 3

units.title: Convex Hulls in Three Dimensions
units.focus: Polyhedra, 3D hull algorithms, boundary representations, randomized incremental hulls, and higher dimensions.
units.label: Chapter 4

units.title: Voronoi Diagrams
units.focus: Voronoi cells, Delaunay triangulations, algorithms, medial axis, hull lifting, and arrangements.
units.label: Chapter 5

units.title: Arrangements
units.focus: Line arrangements, combinatorics, incremental construction, duality, higher-order Voronoi diagrams, and applications.
units.label: Chapter 6

units.title: Search and Intersection
units.focus: Segment, triangle, polygon, polyhedron, convex polygon intersection, point location, and extremal queries.
units.label: Chapter 7

units.title: Motion Planning
units.focus: Shortest paths, disk and polygon translation, ladder motion, robot arms, configuration spaces, and separability.
units.label: Chapter 8

units.title: Sources
units.focus: Bibliographies, FAQs, textbooks, monographs, journals, and conference resources mapped as a computational geometry reading graph.
units.label: Chapter 9
